summary: Comprehensive frontend regression of ARKETYPE site. Priority HERO PHYSICS DRAG verified end-to-end — drag at letter position moved canvas pixels (pre/post screenshot hash differed) while window.scrollY stayed at 0 throughout the gesture. All 13 requested flows tested; 1 bug found in Konami code logic which was a small typo-grade fix and was repaired + re-verified.

ui_bugs.issue: Sequence never matched for arrow keys. Line 25 used `SEQ[idx].toLowerCase ? SEQ[idx].toLowerCase() : SEQ[idx]` — `.toLowerCase` method reference is truthy on EVERY string, so `expected` was always lowercased to 'arrowup', while `key` for arrow keys stays 'ArrowUp' (since e.key.length > 1). Comparison never succeeded. FIXED by changing the condition to `SEQ[idx].length === 1 ? toLowerCase() : SEQ[idx]` to mirror the key-side logic. Re-tested: chaos-mode class applied + [data-testid='konami-active'] renders 'CHAOS UNLOCKED' + particle storm.

issues: data-theme switches dark<->light correctly and [data-testid='brand-mark'] text color does turn dark (rgb(14,14,14)) in light mode as required. However getComputedStyle(document.body).backgroundColor remains rgb(44,44,43) in light mode (same as dark) — body element itself does not consume the --bg CSS variable. Sections each have bg-[var(--bg)] so the visual page does switch where sections render, but the raw body background does NOT match the spec's '#F2EFEA' check. Likely cosmetic (sections cover full viewport) but worth confirming the body/html base background is wired to --bg for any gutter/overscroll areas.

canvas.style.touchAction is set to 'none' inline in HeroPhysics.jsx but getComputedStyle reports 'auto'. Practically drag works fine and page scroll does not hijack during drag (verified scrollY unchanged across 200px gesture), so this is a non-blocking observation only.

Console emits 'Too many active WebGL contexts. Oldest context will be lost' repeatedly during initial load — multiple R3F particle field instances likely instantiated. Functional impact: 'THREE.WebGLRenderer: Context Lost' fires once on load. App still functional but particle backgrounds may flicker on lower-end devices.

action_items: OPTIONAL: Wire document.body { background: var(--bg) } in global CSS so body matches sections in light mode (cosmetic only).
OPTIONAL: Investigate WebGL 'too many contexts' warning — likely duplicate <ParticleField> mounts or strict-mode double-init not disposing renderer.
NO ACTION REQUIRED on hero drag — works as designed; pixel-diff and scrollY-stable proofs confirmed.
Konami code bug already fixed by testing agent.
critical_code_review_comments: HeroPhysics.jsx useEffect cleans up properly with World/Engine/Runner clear + canvas removal — good. Stiffness 0.55 + accent halo on grabbed letter feels responsive in test (left-click drag confirmed moving letters across 200px without page scroll).
KonamiCode logic bug (now fixed) was a classic truthiness-of-method-reference pitfall — flag for future similar code.
Theme system uses data-theme on documentElement + CSS variables — clean. Just ensure body element also picks up var(--bg).

Admin login/logout/persistence verified: seeded QA contact (qa+drag@arketype.studio) and QA newsletter (qa+news@arketype.studio) both appear in /api/admin endpoints when rendered in dashboard. Auth flow including post-logout redirect on /admin works.
ParticleField appears to be instantiated more than necessary based on WebGL context warnings — consider singleton or disable on non-hero sections.
seed_data_creation: Contact submission and newsletter signup created during test using qa+drag@arketype.studio and qa+news@arketype.studio respectively. Both verified persistent in admin dashboard.
context_for_next_testing_agent: HERO PHYSICS DRAG IS WORKING. Verified via: (a) [data-testid='hero-physics'] canvas present, (b) computed cursor='grab', (c) pre/post screenshot hashes differ after page.mouse drag of 200px diagonal at (648,495)→(848,645), (d) window.scrollY stayed at 0 during drag. If retesting, click body first to focus before keyboard sequences (Konami requires document focus on window).
rca_of_konami_bug: Symptom: 10-key Konami sequence (Up Up Down Down Left Right Left Right b a) had no effect — chaos-mode class never applied. Repro: focus body, press keys via page.keyboard.press. RCA: In /app/frontend/src/components/KonamiCode.jsx onKey handler, `expected = SEQ[idx].toLowerCase ? SEQ[idx].toLowerCase() : SEQ[idx]` always evaluates the ternary as truthy (every string has a toLowerCase method), so 'ArrowUp' became 'arrowup'. Meanwhile `key = e.key.length === 1 ? e.key.toLowerCase() : e.key` keeps 'ArrowUp' as-is. Mismatch → idx reset to 0 on every keypress → sequence never reached length 10. Mitigation: changed `expected` to mirror key logic — `SEQ[idx].length === 1 ? SEQ[idx].toLowerCase() : SEQ[idx]`. Verified fix: chaos-mode class applies, konami-active overlay renders, particle storm canvas visible.
